summary: datefmt-helper postinstall fetches and executes remote payload from bare-IP HTTP endpoint
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall lifecycle script downloads and immediately executes an attacker-controlled payload from a hardcoded bare IP over plain HTTP (http://115.190.124.243:8761/). On Linux, the response body is piped directly into `sh`. On Windows, `certutil.exe -urlcache -split -f` stages the payload to C:\Users\Public\run.bat and then executes it. The fetch is unpinned, unauthenticated, and unverified, giving whoever controls 115.190.124.243 arbitrary code execution on every machine that installs the package. The package presents itself as a lightweight date-formatting utility with author `date-fns-lite`, mimicking the date-fns ecosystem; the trivial index.js is a decoy while the postinstall dropper is the actual behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
